candidates.stmt:
Abdul Kadir has lived on Baffins Road for more than 30 years. His children and grandchildren use local schools and he runs a much-loved business on Albert Road.

Since Kadir became a councillor, he has helped secure money for a new ballcourt at Baffins Pond, run litterpicks at Tamworth and Cobden Parks and Great Salterns, dealt with anti-social behaviour on Milton Common and worked with people on traffic problems on Tangier Road and Moorings Way.

He got Southern Water to offer compensation to local businesses affected by its Eastern Road sewer works and launched monthly lunches for old and vulnerable people. He also ran a charity dinner for a local church.

“People across our area know that the local Lib Dem team - Darren Sanders, Leonie Oliver and myself - get things done,” says Kadir. “We work hard for local people all-year-round… and don’t just turn up at election time.”

**People across our area know that Suzy Horton, Kate Dorrington and the local Lib
Dem team get things done. Kate and Suzy have a reputation for listening to residents.**

They work hard for local people all-year-round...and don’t just turn up at election time!

Kate runs a local charity in Southsea helping people who struggle with alcohol and substance misuse.

“Being a councillor is about rolling your sleeves up and being an effective voice,” says Kate.

Suzy, Kate and the Lib Dem councillors secured a £300,000 investment into the Kings Theatre. This will create a new entrance area for customers, an improved box office and a lift. Reform councillors refused to support this investment.

“When the elections come in May, please consider lending me your support to carry on the hard work.

“I will always listen to you and put our area first,” says Kate.

**Kate lives here and has a REAL record of service and local action**

Kate cares about our area. She lives in Southsea and has a record of local action. She knows our area like the back of her hand.

Kate and Suzy has built up a positive reputation as people who help and support local people.

**Kate Dorrington works hard for you**

Kate is a community champion. She has been keen to help Suzy Horton in supporting the local community centre and a local greening group.

She also runs a local charity supporting really vulnerable people in our local area.

This is my first time standing as a candidate but don’t be put off. I simply couldn’t keep watching what’s happening in this country without doing something about it. I can’t remember a time when people were more skint, yet we have more billionaires than ever! We must address this cost-of-living crisis which is leading to increases in crime and anti-social behaviour in Portsmouth. As a business owner, I know we must support local businesses and start-ups.

And we must clean up our city in every meaning of the word – ending fly-tipping, reducing air pollution, and providing real support for people experiencing homelessness. I’m also determined to hold Southern Water to account for years of pollution, and with a proven background in campaigning I quite fancy our chances.

Portsmouth is my home. I care deeply about this City and the people who live here.

**Harold McGregor-Sims grew up in Buckland and went to St Edmund's School in Landport.**

Since then he has set-up two local businesses that employ local people. He now wants to put something back into the community where he grew up.

He has been asked to stand for election by the local Lib Dems.

“Being a councillor is about rolling your sleeves up and being an effective voice,” says Harold.

Harold is campaigning for more CCTV in Council blocks across Buckland, Portsea, Somerstown, Landport and the city centre. He has raised this issue with the Leader of
the Council.

“I want to up a strong voice for local people. When the elections come in May, please consider lending me your support so that I can make sure our area is heard.

“I will always listen to you and always put our area first,” says Harold McGregor-Sims.

**Harold grew up here and has a REAL record of service and local action**

Harold cares about our area. He grew up in Buckland and has a record of local action.
He knows the area like the back of his hand. Harold is working to improve our estates. He has built up a positive reputation as someone who helps and supports local people.

**Harold McGregor-Sims works hard for you**

Harold is a community champion. He has campaigned for repairs and CCTV in council blocks and to clear-up estates, so they are places we can be proud of.

It's evident that traditional politics has failed to address the pressing issues we face today, leaving our communities longing for hope, ambition, and a commitment to a fairer, greener society. As a young queer person with a disability, I am puzzled by the lack of progress and the harmful, backward policies that persist. I want more for our country, I want better for Portsmouth South. I am confident that the Green Party offers a vision for real change amoungst our communities. But what change is needed? Real consideration for our environment, investment into health and social care, a commitment to seeing out global peace, electoral reform and promoting inclusion & diversity within society. There is no doubt this is a big task, yet I know politics done differently like this with a Green MP is up for the task. I am proud to be taking on this challenge in the city I have made home. Let's Turn Pompey Green Together!

Hello people of Copnor. My name is Tamara Groen and I’m your Green Party candidate for the local elections. You may know me from my volunteering at the Repair Cafe/Library of Things in The Cascades. I’m one of the folks that gets you booked in for your repair slot or helps you check out an object to borrow. When I’m not there, I’m busy working with young people as a supported lodgings carer, helping care-experienced young people to thrive in our community.
If elected, I would be keen to support refugees and asylum seekers who arrive here to fight back against the hostile immigration system because, in my professional experience, they help make this island better. Another of my priorities would be to improve public transport and cycle routes across the city, so that we can move around safer, cheaper, and cleaner.

Paul Oakley-Cleife lives in Copnor and has stood as a candidate to be a councillor to Portsmouth City Council before.

Paul has volunteered with Portsmouth Association for the Blind.

He is well known in the area with his guide dog Gabby.

Paul supports the Lib Dem led council being financially stable unlike both Conservative Hampshire and Labour Southampton, both of which are looking at bankruptcy.

Working, living and supporting a breadth of voluntary organisations provides me with a wide range of experience, knowledge and understanding of the local issues and needs of the people of Cosham. As a champion of community engagement, I wants to use my skills and experience to benefit the residents of Cosham and make living here good for everyone.

Timothy Suffolk has lived in Cosham for many years.

He is the Chair of the Portsmouth Birthplace Branch of the Dickens Fellowship.

Timothy has led many educational initiatives both locally and internationally to encourage people to read the works of Charles Dickens, who was born in Portsmouth.

Samantha Darragh lives in Farlington. Her son attends a school in Portsmouth.

Sam has experience of being a councillor in the past.

She supports Portsmouth's bid to stay on its own and not be merged in with other councils to form a monster-council in south-east Hampshire.

If this goes ahead, it will mean that Portsmouth would have to cut millions of pounds of local services on the first day the new council starts, as it would inherit a £67 million deficit from Conservative-led Hampshire County Council. Things like community wardens, libraries, sports facilities, the youth service, the carers service, and more, will face cuts and closure. This election people can send Keir Starmer and Labour HQ a message – Hands Off Portsmouth!

**People across our area know that Matthew Winnington, Peter Candlish, Nick Dorrington and the local Lib Dem team get things done.**

Matthew, Peter and Nick have a reputation for listening to residents.

They work hard for local people all-year-round...and don’t just turn up at election time like the others.

“Being a councillor is about rolling your sleeves up and being an effective voice,” says Matthew.

Matthew is at the heart of decision making in the city and serves on the city’s cabinet. He has been working to make sure that local care services are protected from cuts.

Matthew worked hard to ensure that our area will be getting a new GP surgery in Eastney, and brand new swimming pools and leisure facilities.

“When the elections come in May, please consider lending me your support to carry on the hard work.

“I will always listen to you and always put our area first,” says
Matthew Winnington.

**Matthew lives here and has a REAL record of service and local action**

Matthew cares about our area. He lives in Southsea and has a record of local action.
He knows the area like the back of his hand.

As your local councillor for 13 years, Matthew has built up a positive reputation as someone who helps and supports local people.

Billy was born and brought up in Portsmouth. He lives in Fratton and works for an engineering firm on the Eastern Road. He has a large local family and knows the city well.

Billy, Cllr Dave Ashmore and Cllr Stuart Brown listened to local people when they asked for help with parking. Now there are Residents Parking Schemes across Fratton because of the Lib Dem councillors.

**Billy lives here and has a record of service and action in the city**

Billy cares about our area. He lives in Fratton and has a record of local action. Billy helped to get outside funding for the D-Day commemorations for the city.

He knows the area like the back of his hand. Billy has built up a positive reputation as
someone who helps and supports local people.

**Billy Ansell works hard for you**

Billy conducted the largest survey on local NHS and health care issues in Fratton. He is campaigning for more NHS dentists.

Hello, I’m Jacob Short, I’m a local lad living in Fratton, I attended a local secondary school and went to Portsmouth College where I studied Law, Politics and Digitial Media/Journalism.

I have decided to stand to be a local voice on a national stage, without influence from national parties and to be able to represent the constituents of Portsmouth South without external pressures from party whips. No towing the party line.

Portsmouth is an amazing city with a wide range of diverse cultures and communities, with amazing local businesses and charities. But, we are also facing many issues that need to be addressed by a strong, active and committed local person.

Our local infrastructure is falling apart. Housing issues and homelessness is on the increase, our roads are crumbling with potholes everywhere and people cannot access the basics like a dentist or GP. Then there’s the shop lifting, anti social behaviour, joyriding across the city. The dumping of sewage into our seas is another strong issue being known as a “waterfront city”, why can’t we swim in our seas?

Vote for a local person who puts Portsmouth South first and foremost. Vote for change. Vote Portsmouth Independents.

Tim is a local resident and has been an active campaigner in Fratton for the last few years,  with his ancestral routes tracing back to nearby Emsworth. 

Tim works as a software contractor and AI specialist. In his career, he has been involved in problem solving and delivery of solutions in complex research and engineering projects. His understanding of evidence based policy is needed to address the significant challenges we face.

After seeing worrying signs of environmental and social break down, Tim decided to get involved in politics and local campaigns. He has had a long interest in environmental issues, civil rights and membership of the EU. Tim currently specializes in air quality and sustainable transport issues. He has volunteered with Portsmouth Foodcycle and Fratton Big Local to help support the local community.

"We need to abandon old dogmatic solutions in favour of sustainable and accountable systems. Of particular concern in Portsmouth is the level of traffic, which causes parking problems, delays, pollution and climate change. I will fight for more investment in cycling, walking and public transport, as well as requiring development schemes to put these priorities at their centre. Green spaces need to be introduced around the city to improve well being. The city also needs to improve its recycling rates, which are among the worst in the country.

I am an air quality campaigner and regular blogger on the #LetPompeyBreathe campaign and I support efforts to remain in the EU."

Emma is a long-time Green Party member with a strong passion for social justice, which she knows is tied to the climate crisis and economic justice.

She has lived in Hilsea her entire life, so she’s seen how the north of the city has been **let down** by Portsmouth City Council, no matter which of the other parties on the ballot have been in power.

As your councillor, Emma would insist our **council taxes are distributed fairly**, to reduce inequality and improve services in underfunded wards.

She will also fight to ensure that **flood defences are strengthened** throughout the city to ensure our homes are protected for generations to come, campaign to lower our bills and carbon footprint by retrofitting properties across Portsmouth, and make it easier to recycle by making all recycling kerbside collections (including glass).

Born and raised locally, lam proud to come from a family with many generations having lived in the area. My family has links to the Royal Navy, Army, Royal Air Force and Ministry of Defence. I work locally in the fitness and leisure industry.

Kristine works in the beauty industry in North End and is soon to become the mother of her first child.

She is an active local campaigner and supports the Hands Off Portsmouth campaign.

Steve has been a councillor for 9 years, the last four in Milton Ward, where he lives. He is the elected Leader of Portsmouth City Council.

For many years, Steve ran the Cellars at Eastney. He champions all things ‘arts and culture’ for the city, and has ensured that Portsmouth has a year of celebrations to commemorate its 100th anniversary as a city.

Steve, alongside his Lib Dem ward colleagues Kimberly Barrett and Gerald Vernon-Jackson, has built up a positive reputation as someone who helps and supports local people.

Steve and the local Lib Dem councillors have made sure the council is well-led. Portsmouth has lower council tax than nearby cities. Unlike other areas, the city provides a weekly bin collection service and isn’t cutting services.

Investment by the Lib Dem-led council will also see a brand new GP surgery and swimming pools in Eastney and Steve has led this project.

I’ve spent years working in education, supporting local arts, and campaigning at the grassroots level, standing up for the things that make our community thrive.

I grew up in Milton, and I care deeply about protecting what makes it special. I’ve fought to save local buildings and green spaces, and campaigned against destructive developments, both locally and nationally, that threaten our environment and identity. I’m passionate about protecting wildlife and tackling sewage dumping, air pollution, and rising rents that are pushing people out of our community.

As a musician, I understand how vital the arts are, not just culturally, but economically, and I’ll always champion this sector. Through my work in schools, I see first-hand how important strong education and SEND support are for our children’s future. They deserve better, and so do our communities.
I was proud to stand as a Labour candidate and remain grateful for that time, but I now believe the Green Party best reflects my long standing values.

Milton deserves a representative who listens, takes action, and delivers practical solutions, protecting our green spaces, supporting local services, the arts, and building a better future for everyone.

Steve served in the Royal Navy for over 20 years and now works for SERCO in the dockyard commissioning new tug boats.

**Steve Andrews lives here and has a record of local service and action**

Steve cares about our area. He lives just off Northern Parade, and has a record of local action over many years. He knows the area like the back of his hand. Popular local  councillor Lee Hunt is backing Steve Andrews.

**Steve Andrews works hard for you**
Steve is a community champion. He has been an active volunteer for many years at the Buckland Play Activity Centre and cares about our area.

Michelle and her family live in the heart of Paulsgrove. Her kids go to local schools.

She works at the hospital and cares about the NHS.

Michelle stood in the Paulsgrove by-election late last year and nearly tripled the Lib Dem vote.

"I am seeking election to Portsmouth City Council as I believe the Green Party offers a new approach to local government, which many people will welcome. Sustainability is an increasingly important factor in decisions made by local and national government, but is continually side-stepped in favour of short-term financial issues, putting problems off for a few years. We must take sustainability into account when we make decisions now, for example requiring all large developments and all public buildings to generate at least 10% of their own energy on site and constructing all seafront properties to withstand current and future levels of flooding.
Portsmouth is an island naturally suited for cycling, yet it is one of the most dangerous places in the country to do so. We have one of the worst rates of childhood obesity, areas of the city that continually fail air quality standards, and terrible traffic and parking problems. Making the City safer and more attractive for cyclists represents an instant win on all these fronts, yet the council continues to pay no more than lip service to the idea.

We are seeing the last of the Island's green spaces increasingly sought by developers to build houses, while we have far too many empty properties and underutilized brownfield sites. We have to stop building dormitory developments for people who commute to jobs elsewhere, and start developing more affordable properties for small businesses and start-ups, with particular support for co-operatives and social enterprises. Our local shopping areas are dying, with shops closing and new businesses unable to find the money for high rents and business rates.

Labour, the LibDems and the Conservatives are all committed to increasing the privatization of public services in the name of "leaner" government. As a Green, I believe in investment in real jobs, supporting local businesses instead of minimum wage-paying chains based in tax havens. I believe in local accountability for school

People across our area know that Denise Perry, Councillor Hugh Mason and the local Lib Dem team get things done.

They have a reputation for listening to residents. The local Lib Dem team work hard for local people all-year-round...and don’t just turn up at election time!

Lib Dem councillors have made sure that £300,000 is being invested to the
Kings Theatre to build a new entrance area, box office and lift.

Denise has been selected by local Lib Dems as their candidate to be your next councillor.

Denise worked in the NHS for many years. She brings a wealth of experience in
getting councils and the NHS working closer together.

“Being a councillor is about rolling your sleeves up and being an
effective voice,” says Denise.

“When the elections come in May, please consider lending me your
support to carry on the hard work.

“I will always listen to you and always put our area first,” says Denise Perry.

**Denise lives here and has a record of service and action**

Denise cares about our area. She lives in Southsea and has a record of local action. She knows the area like the back of her hand.

For many years Denise worked in the NHS. She is a campaigner for better health services.

**Denise Perry works hard for you**

Denise is a community champion. She holds weekly advice surgeries with Lib Dem councillor Hugh Mason at Southsea Library and is building a positive reputation as someone who helps and supports local people.

Alvie is committed to ensuring Portsmouth is a city that everyone feels comfortable and proud to call their home. Born and brought up in Portsmouth, he has witnessed how local government decisions affect the city he loves.

Alvie has campaigned with local groups involved with increasing community cohesion, rejecting racism and identity-based hatred, and advocating for local and international human rights issues – such as in Palestine – to be recognised and resolved.

Since graduating from the University of Portsmouth, he has worked as a data engineer on environmental monitoring sensors, enabling companies and local authorities to make more informed decisions – and prevent pollution, flooding, and expensive maintenance.

As your councillor, he would be a passionate and reliable voice for St Jude’s, making sure that resident concerns are duly represented and considered in council, and that Portsmouth is a welcoming and tolerant city for all.

**People across our area know that Mark Jeffery, Chris Attwell, Ian Holder and the local Lib Dem team get things done.**

They work hard for local people all-year-round...and don’t just turn up at election time like the others.

Mark, Chris and Ian hold regular advice surgeries in Old Portsmouth and Somerstown.

He is at the heart of decision-making in the Lib Dem Group leading Portsmouth City Council.

“Being a councillor is about rolling your sleeves up and being an effective voice,”
says Mark.

Mark has worked hard to ensure that Somerstown and Castle Road are
getting significant investment.

He is a member of Somers Orchard Community Panel and attends Castle Road Community Association and Old Portsmouth & Gunwharf Quays Neighbourhood Forum meetings.

Mark recently chaired the panel that scrutinises local NHS and health services for residents in Portsmouth.

“When the elections come in May, please consider lending me your support to carry on our hard work.

“I will always listen to you and always put our area first,” says Mark Jeffery.

Committed. Generous. Energetic. That’s what Portsmouth Green Party members said when we asked them to describe our candidate for St Thomas Ward, Jay Mercer.

While Jay may be young at just 23, they know what many in Westminster fail to grasp after decades in power; politics is all about helping people.

Jay has met many of you on the doorstop for chats about your concerns and was excited and energised by the connections they made.

They’ve made a home in Portsmouth with their partner and intends to make things better for our community with safer streets, reduced air pollution, and action on the climate crisis; reaching across the aisle for a bipartisan solution. After all, as Jay often says, it’s not important who gets the credit, only who gets the help they need.

Jay said: “I will fight for improved housing, affordable bus fares and inclusivity within our community.“
